chapter_context.summary: Extols the value of a good name and learning from sorrow, advises balance and avoiding moral extremes, and laments the profound difficulty of finding true wisdom.

outline.text: The Value of a Good Name, Mourning over Feasting, and Wisdom's Rebuke
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Value of a Good Name, Mourning over Feasting, and Wisdom's Rebuke (7:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ecclesiastes.

outline.text: Patient Wisdom vs. Angry Folly; Prosperity and Adversity of God's Design
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Patient Wisdom vs. Angry Folly; Prosperity and Adversity of God's Design (7:7-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ecclesiastes.

outline.text: Avoiding Extremes of Over-Righteousness or Over-Wickedness; Wisdom's Shield
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Avoiding Extremes of Over-Righteousness or Over-Wickedness; Wisdom's Shield (7:15-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ecclesiastes.

outline.text: The Difficulty of Finding Wisdom; The Preacher's Reflections
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Difficulty of Finding Wisdom; The Preacher's Reflections (7:23-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ecclesiastes.

key_themes.title: Life Under the Sun
key_themes.description: The phrase 'under the sun' refers to the purely horizontal, physical world, highlighting the vanity of seeking ultimate meaning in mortal pursuits alone.

key_themes.title: The Gift of the Present
key_themes.description: Despite the vanities, the Preacher consistently advises rejoicing in the present moment, loving families, enjoying food, and taking pride in one's labor as direct gifts from God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the search of Chapter 7, in what ways have you sought ultimate fulfillment in 'vanity' (pleasures, wealth, achievements) rather than in God?
How does this chapter encourage you to slow down, enjoy your daily portion, and trust in the sovereign timing of the Creator?

cultural_historical.title: Qoheleth (The Preacher)
cultural_historical.text: The author calls himself Qoheleth, traditionally translated as 'The Preacher' or 'The Assembly Leader', exploring deep philosophical and theological struggles in late Old Testament history.
